message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To avoid exhaustion of medical resources by COVID-19 care, policy-makers must predict care needs, specifically estimating the proportion of severe cases likely to require intensive care. In Osaka prefecture, Japan, the number of these severe cases exceeded the capacity of ICU units prepared for COVID-19 from mid-April, 2021.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study used a statistical model to elucidate dynamics of severe cases in Osaka and validated the model through prospective testing.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The study extended from April 3, 2020 through April 26, 2021 in Osaka prefecture, Japan prefecture. We regressed the number of severe cases on the number of severe cases the day prior and the newly onset patients of more than 21 days prior.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We selected the number of severe cases the day prior and the number of newly onset patients on 21 and 28 days prior as explanatory variables for explaining the number of severe cases based on the adjusted determinant coefficient. The adjusted coefficient of determination was greater than 0.995 and indicated good fit. Prospective out of sample three-week prediction forecast the peak date precisely, but the level was not t.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion and Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A reason for the gap in the prospective prediction might be the emergence of variant strains.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Prediction of severe COVID-19 cases requiring intensive care in Osaka, Japan

reference.unstructured: Nippon Housou Kyoukai. Prof. Nishiura of Kyoto university estimated that the number of severe cases in Osaka prefecture be higher than 500 at the end of April and required urgent response. https://www3.nhk.or.jp/news/html/20210415/k10012976211000.html. (in Japanese) [accessed on May 24, 2021]
Osaka Prefectural Government. The Situation of Monitored Items in Osaka Model. http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/iryo/osakakansensho/corona_model.html (in Japanese) [accessed on April 29, 2021]

Sugishita Y , Kurita J , Sugawara T , Ohkusa Y. Effects of voluntary event cancellation and school closure as countermeasures against COVID-19 outbreak in Japan. Plos one 2020. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0239455

reference.article-title: Short Term Prediction of Infectious Diseases Patients from Prescription Surveillance
